topic: Mix Review diagnosis and fix policy

rules.recommendation: Treat Master peaks near or above 0 dBFS as output/render clipping or headroom risk and make them high priority.
rules.rationale: The Master feeds fixed-bit-depth render/output paths where sustained clipping can become destructive.
safety_limits: Use measured peak/readback data where available.
Keep diagnosis read-only; writes require explicit user approval.

rules.recommendation: Treat insert-track peaks above 0 dBFS as headroom, balance, or stem-render risk rather than the same failure class as Master/output clipping.
rules.rationale: FL Studio can process internally in floating point, but hot inserts still reduce practical headroom and can make downstream processing or stems risky.
safety_limits: Do not call insert-track peaks output clipping unless the Master/output path also clips.
Prefer source evidence and exact peak values in the finding.

rules.recommendation: Prefer source-track or bus trims before treating a Master fader reduction as the default fix.
rules.rationale: Source trims improve balance and lower the Master load without shrinking the whole mix indiscriminately.
safety_limits: Apply one approved rollback-backed trim or one named grouped rollback unit at a time.
Avoid applying source trims and a Master alternative blindly together.

rules.recommendation: EQ, compression, limiting, reverb, delay, and mastering suggestions may configure already-loaded plugins only through safe wrappers.
rules.rationale: Plugin loading and render/mastering automation are out of scope for the current MCP safety contract.
safety_limits: Do not write uncalibrated plugin parameters.
Do not claim render, save, or FL Cloud Mastering automation.

rules.recommendation: Keep Low-End/Stereo Safety Assistant behavior read-only; report mixer pan, mixer stereo separation, low-end layering, and Master headroom as review findings or manual checks rather than automatic fixes.
rules.rationale: Current MCP reads mixer pan, stereo separation metadata, track names, and peaks, but it does not expose true phase correlation, mono-sum cancellation, or spectral sub-band width.
safety_limits: Do not claim true phase correlation, mono-sum cancellation, or sub-spectrum width from mixer metadata alone.
Do not apply stereo separation, mid-side EQ, plugin loading, rendering, saving, or mastering automation from this assistant.
Use playback or full-song watch peaks for hot low-end and Master-headroom findings when level evidence matters.
